mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding one percentage point signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 1.0pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return frequency above 58% over 60 days, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a sustained decline in the industrial production index reflects constrained supply-side capacity and weakening economic momentum, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of observations during tightening cycles when industrial production declines.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days as market participants price in higher corporate revenues.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a frequency exceeding 55% across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond a 0.5 percent threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPI inflation exceeds the 0.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales expansion confirm broad-based demand strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days as equity valuations expand on improved earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal yields a negative return frequency above 50% during expansion phases, invalidating the demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Following large-scale fiscal stimulus deployment, rising industrial production signals rapid supply chain normalization and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations following stimulus announcements when industrial production rises.
